prompt:
You are running autonomous security audit pass {run_index} with {agent_name} against this repository: {project_dir}.

Goal: find real security problems, weak assumptions, missing access gates, auth/authz mistakes, unsafe networking or deployment behavior, dependency/package risks, secret-handling issues, and weaknesses that are commonly introduced by AI-generated code.

Before starting, read any previous audit files under {audit_dir} if they exist. Briefly account for what was already covered, then try a different angle so this pass adds new value instead of repeating prior findings.

Write exactly two markdown files:
- {audit_dir}/run-{run_index}-{agent_name}-findings.md
- {audit_dir}/run-{run_index}-{agent_name}-fix-plan.md

The findings file should prioritize concrete, code-referenced issues with severity, exploitability, affected files, and confidence. The fix-plan file should give an actionable remediation plan ordered by risk and dependency. If you find no strong issue in an area, say what you checked and why it looks acceptable. Do not make broad claims without code evidence.
